questionText: Which country is Gotye from?

questionText: What is INXS frontman Michael Hutchence's tragic claim to fame beyond music?
funFact: INXS continued for years after Hutchence's death, even holding a reality TV show to find a new singer — nothing could replace his charisma

questionText: Who is the multi-instrumentalist behind Tame Impala?
funFact: Kevin Parker records everything alone in his home studio — the 'band' only exists for live shows. He plays every instrument on the albums

questionText: Which country is Django Reinhardt from?
funFact: Django Reinhardt lost the use of two fingers in a fire at age 18 — he reinvented his guitar technique with just two working fingers and became the greatest jazz guitarist ever

questionText: Which Brazilian artist reinterpreted David Bowie songs in Portuguese for 'The Life Aquatic'?
funFact: Seu Jorge's Portuguese Bowie covers for Wes Anderson's film were so beloved that Bowie himself said 'had Seu Jorge not recorded my songs in Portuguese, I would never have heard this new level of beauty'

questionText: Which country is Sepultura from?
funFact: Sepultura's 'Roots' album incorporated indigenous Xavante tribal music recorded in the Amazon — blending extreme metal with Brazilian indigenous culture

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Arcade Fire?
funFact: Win Butler and Régine Chassagne are married — she's also a multi-instrumentalist who plays accordion, drums, keyboards, and hurdy-gurdy in the band

questionText: What is Bryan Adams' longest-running #1 single?
correctAnswer: (Everything I Do) I Do It for You — 16 weeks
options: (Everything I Do) I Do It for You — 16 weeks
Have You Ever Really Loved a Woman? — 10 weeks

backgroundSongName: (Everything I Do) I Do It for You

questionText: Which film features 'Hallelujah' by Leonard Cohen in an iconic scene?
funFact: Leonard Cohen wrote 80 draft verses of 'Hallelujah' over 5 years — it was initially a flop, but after Jeff Buckley's cover and its use in Shrek, it became one of the most-covered songs ever

questionText: Which TV series features 'Where Is My Mind?' by Pixies prominently throughout?
funFact: Mr. Robot used 'Where Is My Mind?' as a deliberate nod to Fight Club — both stories deal with alter egos and the destruction of capitalism

questionText: Which country is Céline Dion from?
funFact: Céline Dion's manager René Angélil mortgaged his house to fund her first album when she was 12 — they later married when she was 26 and he was 52

questionText: What makes Daniel Caesar's Grammy-winning album 'Freudian' unique?
options: It was recorded in one day
It was his debut and final album
funFact: Daniel Caesar turned down every major label and self-released 'Freudian' — it won a Grammy for Best R&B Performance, proving indie artists can compete at the highest level

questionText: Which country is Devin Townsend from?
funFact: Devin Townsend is known for his extreme versatility — he's made everything from brutal death metal to new age ambient music, sometimes on the same album

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Paramore?
funFact: Hayley Williams was only 16 when Paramore released their debut — she's one of the youngest artists to score a #1 hit on Billboard Alternative

questionText: Which film features 'Old Town Road' by Lil Nas X?
correctAnswer: None — it became famous through TikTok, not a film

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
funFact: 'Old Town Road' spent 19 weeks at #1, breaking the record held by 'Despacito' — it started as a $30 beat bought online and a TikTok meme

questionText: Which country is Anitta from?
funFact: Anitta grew up in a favela in Rio de Janeiro — she became the first Brazilian solo artist to reach #1 on Spotify Global with 'Envolver'

questionText: What is Neil Young's nickname?
options: Both The Godfather of Grunge and Old Shakey
funFact: Kurt Cobain quoted Neil Young's lyric 'it's better to burn out than to fade away' in his suicide note — Young said it was 'the most disturbing thing about the whole thing'

questionText: Which TV series features a theme by Bear McCreary with war drums and Celtic pipes?
funFact: Bear McCreary used Robert Louis Stevenson's 'Skye Boat Song' as the basis for the Outlander theme — adapting a centuries-old Scottish folk song for modern TV

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Blondie?
funFact: Many people think 'Blondie' is Debbie Harry's name — she once said 'my name isn't Blondie, Blondie is a group' but the confusion persists to this day

questionText: Which country is Celia Cruz from?
funFact: Celia Cruz was exiled from Cuba in 1960 and never returned — Fidel Castro personally banned her from entering the country, but she became the 'Queen of Salsa' worldwide

questionText: What is Billie Joe Armstrong from Green Day's side project called?

questionText: Which Jordan Peele film features 'I Got 5 on It' by Luniz in a terrifying context?

questionText: Which country is J Balvin from?
funFact: J Balvin is called 'The Prince of Reggaeton' — he was the most-streamed Latin artist on Spotify for multiple years running

questionText: Which TV series features a whistled theme that became iconic for Westerns?

Most weeks at #1 for a debut
First song to reach 1 billion streams in a week

questionText: Which Guardians of the Galaxy features 'Come and Get Your Love' by Redbone?

questionText: Which animated show features a theme by The Dandy Warhols?

questionText: Which rapper's real name is Calvin Broadus Jr.?

questionText: Which Pixar film features 'Remember Me' as its central theme?

questionText: Which Danish TV series features the theme 'A Drifting Life' by Trentemøller?

questionText: Which TV series features 'Take Me Home, Country Roads' by John Denver in a post-apocalyptic context?

questionText: Which sitcom features a theme written and performed by the Rembrandts that starts with four hand claps?

questionText: Who founded Gorillaz with Damon Albarn?

questionText: What language does Sigur Rós often sing in?

questionText: Which country is Bonobo from?

questionText: Which HBO series about a dysfunctional wealthy family features a theme by Nicholas Britell?
funFact: The Succession theme mixes classical piano with hip-hop beats — Nicholas Britell originally composed hip-hop beats for Natalie Portman before scoring prestige TV

questionText: What is the real name of the artist known as The Weeknd?

questionText: Which Marvel film features 'Immigrant Song' by Led Zeppelin in an epic fight scene?

questionText: Which country is Gojira from?

questionText: Who is the DJ and producer behind The Chemical Brothers?

questionText: What was Kate Bush's first single that went to #1 when she was just 19?

questionText: Which country is Trentemøller from?
funFact: Trentemøller's debut 'The Last Resort' is considered one of the best electronic albums of the 2000s — he makes dark, cinematic music from his studio in Copenhagen

questionText: Which Netflix series about chess features a score that became a standalone classical hit?

questionText: Which artist's debut album 'The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill' won 5 Grammys?

questionText: Which Guy Ritchie film features 'Disco Science' by Mirwais?

questionText: Which country is Agnes Obel from?
funFact: Agnes Obel composes music using a technique she calls 'prepared piano' — placing objects on piano strings to create otherworldly sounds in her Berlin apartment studio

questionText: Who is the bassist of Muse?

questionText: What does BTS stand for in Korean?
funFact: BTS started from a tiny Korean label with almost no budget — they built their fanbase through free content on social media, becoming the biggest band in the world without traditional Western marketing

questionText: Which country is Tinariwen from?

questionText: Which film features 'A Real Hero' by College & Electric Youth?

questionText: Which Nordic noir TV series features a moody electronic theme by Trentemøller?

questionText: What was Joni Mitchell's real birth name?
funFact: Joni Mitchell gave up a daughter for adoption in 1965 before she was famous — they reunited 32 years later in 1997, and the experience influenced much of her songwriting

questionText: Which country is Ravi Shankar from?
funFact: Ravi Shankar taught George Harrison to play sitar, introducing Indian classical music to the Western world — his daughter Norah Jones also became a multi-Grammy winning artist

questionText: Who is the rapper in Run the Jewels alongside El-P?
funFact: Killer Mike is also a political activist — he gave a famous speech during the 2020 Atlanta protests that was broadcast nationwide, showing hip-hop's political power

questionText: Which Tarantino film features 'Battle Without Honor or Humanity' by Tomoyasu Hotei?
funFact: Tomoyasu Hotei is a Japanese rock guitarist — his instrumental became so associated with Kill Bill that most people think it was written for the film, but it existed since 2000

questionText: Which country is Kim Larsen from?
funFact: Kim Larsen is Denmark's most beloved musician — when he died in 2018, thousands of Danes gathered spontaneously in the streets to sing his songs

questionText: What is the name of Kanye West's mother, who inspired the album 'Donda'?
funFact: Donda West was an English professor who left her tenure to manage Kanye's career — her death in 2007 profoundly affected him and shaped the rest of his artistic output

questionText: Which TV series features 'Life on Mars?' by David Bowie as its title and theme?
options: The Man Who Fell to Earth
funFact: The BBC series 'Life on Mars' was named after the Bowie song because the show's concept — a detective transported to 1973 — felt as alien as the song's lyrics

questionText: Who was the original guitarist of D-A-D (Disneyland After Dark)?

questionText: Which producer created the 'Wall of Sound' technique in the 1960s?
funFact: Phil Spector's Wall of Sound used dozens of musicians playing the same parts simultaneously — the Righteous Brothers' 'You've Lost That Lovin' Feelin'' is considered its masterpiece

questionText: Which country is Vangelis from?

questionText: Which James Cameron film features 'I See You' performed by Leona Lewis?

questionText: Who is the vocalist of Portishead?
funFact: Beth Gibbons suffers from intense stage fright — despite being one of the most acclaimed vocalists in music, she rarely performs live and once said performing makes her physically ill

questionText: What instrument is Yo-Yo Ma famous for playing?
funFact: Yo-Yo Ma once left his $2.5 million Stradivarius cello in a New York taxi — it was returned safely. He's done this with instruments worth millions MULTIPLE times
